page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, featuring 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU allocation is dedicated, and the processor cache hierarchy includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 33 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results place the n2-standard-16 consistently in the average performance tier for most standard operations. It achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1839.48 and a multi-core score of 15060.78. Memory bandwidth is measured at 828.96 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 84.64 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. Performance tradeoffs are evident in single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, where the instance registers weak performance, such as 23.66 GB/sec in gzip compression and 8.69 GB/sec in bzip3 decompression. Similarly, while small-model LLM inference runs at average speeds, medium-model LLM inference is weak, processing prompts at 20.13 tokens/sec and generating text at 7.85 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-16 offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that do not require specialized hardware accelerators or high-speed local NVMe storage. Its average performance in database operations and static web serving throughput makes it appropriate for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, web serving, and moderate database workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with 8 physical cores and hyper-threading, it supports AVX-512 instructions but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks position this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, including a multi-core stress-ng score of 15060.78 and static web serving throughput of 12.34 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weak performance in single-threaded compression and medium-model LLM inference. Given its balanced resource allocation and qualitative cost profile, the instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-16 is a general-purpose instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering balanced average performance for database operations and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-16 is a general-purpose VM with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, delivering balanced average performance for standard workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine offering dedicated Intel Xeon processing and standard memory density for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM on x86_64 Intel Xeon architecture
Supports AVX-512 vector and neural network instructions
Average performance for database operations and static web serving
Weak single-threaded compression and medium-model LLM inference speeds
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Appropriate for software compilation, web serving, and general-purpose applications
